The Son of David, even Jesus Christ, whom the prophet sees with a measuring line in his hand, is the Master-Builder of his church. God notices the extent of his church, and will make sure that whatever number of guests are brought to the wedding supper, there will be room. This vision means well for Jerusalem. The walls of a city, as they defend it, so they restrict its inhabitants; but Jerusalem will be extended as freely as if it had no walls at all, yet will be as safe as if it had the strongest walls. In the church of God there is still room for other multitudes, more than people can number. None will be refused who trust in Christ; and He never shuts out from heaven one true member of the church on earth. God will be a wall of fire around them, which cannot be broken through or undermined, nor can it be attacked without danger to those who attack. This vision was to have its full accomplishment in the gospel church, which is extended by admitting Gentiles into it; and which has the Son of God for its Prince and Protector; especially in the glorious times yet to come.

If God will build Jerusalem for the people and their comfort, they must inhabit it for him and his glory. The promises and privileges with which God’s people are blessed should encourage us to join them, whatever it costs us. When Zion is enlarged to make room for all of God's Israel, it is the greatest madness for any of them to stay in Babylon. The captivity of a sinful state is not to be continued in, even if a person may be comfortable in worldly matters. Escape for your life; do not look behind you. Christ has proclaimed deliverance to the captives, which He has Himself accomplished, and it concerns everyone to resolve that sin shall not have dominion over them. Those who want to be found among God’s children must save themselves from this world, see. What Christ will do for his church will be an evident proof of God's care and affection. Whoever touches you, touches the apple of His eye. This is a strong expression of God's love to his church. He takes what is done against her as done against the tenderest part of the eye, to which the least touch is a great offense. Christ is sent to be the Protector of his church.

Here is a prediction of the coming of Christ in human nature. Many nations in that day will renounce idolatry, and God will accept those for His people who join Him with purpose of heart. Glorious times are foretold as a prophecy of our Lord’s coming and kingdom. God is about to do something unexpected, and very surprising, and to plead his people's cause, which had long seemed neglected. Silently submit to his holy will, and patiently wait the event; assured that God will complete all his work. He will soon come to judgment, to complete the salvation of his people, and to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their sins.
